pages.full_text:
the playground.
		We've had a family ping pong tournament. Simon & I beat everyone, but he beat me.
	__Monday July 19th.__
		We left & went to __Parma__. What with one thing (Peter needing to pee) & another (everyone window shopping) we got to the Duomo just as it shut. We had difficulty finding a lunch place. The boys behaved dreadfully in the pink marble baptistery & cathedral & Eve got fed up & dehydrated. We then saw St Giovanni Evangelista & went back to the van. We camped at __Parma__, where there was an open air concert with a blond singer, but we couldn't go because of the children
	__July 20th.__
		*** The campsite has a resident fun fair. John & Charles & Peter went on a merry-go-round yesterday & all the boys
pages.notes: Page begins mid-sentence ('the playground.') — text continues from the previous spread. 'Duomo' is the word read as 'Duomo' (the cathedral/baptistery complex in Parma). Year not explicit on this page; Parma trip and children's names (Simon, Peter, John, Charles, Eve) are consistent with the family's European camper-van travels in this volume (1989–2003); year cannot be determined from content alone so iso_guess is tentative. 'Suono' was considered but 'Duomo' fits context and Italian usage.

pages.full_text:
trampolined on a 10 bouncer.
		Today we took John & Charles again.
pages.notes: The page contains two lines of text at the top continuing from the left page (July 20th entry), followed by a glued-in colour postcard/photograph of a medieval castle. The caption below the postcard reads 'Torrechiara, south of Parma'. Handwritten page number '16' visible in top-right corner.
